caveats: a: No six-digit task-level exposure measurement was located.
a: Product families differ materially in batch intensity, laboratory content, and sales complexity.
rho: The estimate does not assume autonomous control of hazardous processes.
rho: RMP obligations apply only where regulated substances exceed thresholds, so compliance burden varies.

e: Eligibility can change sharply with environmental diligence and product-specific regulation.

s5: Closures and internal family succession are not qualifying transfers.

q: The code spans proprietary specialties and commodity-like preparations with very different bargaining power.

envelope.interval_scope: research-input sensitivity with dataset anchors (l, n) held fixed

identity.title: All Other Miscellaneous Chemical Product and Preparation Manufacturing

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat unit, batch, toll-formulation, or supply-agreement revenue for niche preparations such as activated carbon, automotive chemicals, foundry chemicals, water-treatment preparations, and other products not classified elsewhere; contracts range from spot orders to qualified recurring programs.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive internal plants, pure distributors, commodity basic chemicals classified elsewhere, pharmaceuticals, pesticides, paints, adhesives, soaps, cosmetics, shells, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market manufacturers and custom formulators in NAICS 325998 that repeatedly supply miscellaneous specialty chemical products or preparations to external industrial, commercial, or consumer-channel customers.

caveats: No six-digit task-level exposure measurement was located.
Product families differ materially in batch intensity, laboratory content, and sales complexity.
a.change_evidence: A current 325998 OEWS staffing matrix joined to representative plant time studies that identify task hours, wage weights, current automation, and feasible AI-assisted redesign by product family.
a.rationale: Batch operation, material handling, maintenance, sampling, and safety-critical process control remain physical. AI is more exposed in scheduling, formulation search, demand planning, quality-pattern recognition, regulatory documentation, procurement, technical service, quoting, and administration. The range is a wage-weighted judgment across a very mixed code.

d5.bridge: American Chemistry Council forecasts measure broad US chemical and specialty-chemical output rather than constant-quality quantity for 325998. The base extrapolates slow near-term industry output into a near-flat-to-modestly-growing five-year miscellaneous-specialty basket. This may overstate product families facing substitution or regulation and understate niches tied to water treatment, electronics, or reshoring.
caveats: Trade-association forecasts are broader than the code and may carry member perspective.
Revenue growth can reflect raw-material prices rather than constant-quality volume.
d5.change_evidence: Census product-class shipment quantities and customer-industry volumes for the principal 325998 products, linked to five-year capacity announcements, substitution trends, and regulatory restrictions.
d5.rationale: Broad chemical output is currently weak, but this code serves many end markets and recurring maintenance or process needs. Diversification supports roughly stable real quantity in the base while product mix and industrial cycles justify a wide range.

caveats: The injected count is model-derived and the residual code is unusually heterogeneous.
Eligibility can change sharply with environmental diligence and product-specific regulation.
e.change_evidence: A verified firm roster with product family, external recurring-revenue share, EBITDA, ownership, customer concentration, permit and remediation status, formulation ownership, registrations, and plant transferability.
e.rationale: Many niche formulators have repeat external customers and separable operations, but the residual NAICS category also contains product businesses with spot demand, captive or diversified plants, commodity exposure, environmental liabilities, customer concentration, and formulas or registrations that may not transfer cleanly.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 325998 ($3,997,055,350) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 325998 ($29,537,123,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1801 (margins.json[325]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Chemical (Specialty) EBITDA/Sales 18.01%); revenue/employee $689,266 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 325998); class <5: 403 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,059,360 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $190,791 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 169 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,599,675 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $468,201 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 157 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,203,172 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,117,191 -> IN $1-10M band; class 20-99: 216 firms, avg revenue/firm $18,224,713 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,282,271 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 73 firms, avg revenue/firm $69,238,671 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $12,469,885 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 94 firms, avg revenue/firm $198,999,968 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $35,839,894 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 373

caveats: Operator requirement is high conditional on physical demand remaining.
Insourcing or offshore substitution can displace a lens operator without eliminating chemical use.
o.change_evidence: Product-level customer interviews and churn records identifying demand eliminated, reformulated away, insourced, imported, or replaced by software-enabled process changes, along with evidence of on-site customer generation.
o.rationale: The surviving demand is for physical chemical products whose formulation, blending, packaging, testing, release, and hazardous-material accountability require an operating manufacturer. Software may reduce technical-service effort or some end uses but cannot independently supply the physical preparation.

caveats: No direct evidence measures retention of AI-created benefit in this code.
The code spans proprietary specialties and commodity-like preparations with very different bargaining power.
q.change_evidence: Representative contract and invoice cohort showing pricing mechanism, raw-material indexation, open-book provisions, rebates, rebid cadence, customer concentration, switching costs, and price realization following documented process savings.
q.rationale: Savings can remain with a manufacturer under unit-priced or formula-based niche products, particularly where qualification, technical service, or proprietary formulations create switching friction. Retention is lower in tolling, cost-plus, indexed, competitively bid, or readily substitutable products, and sophisticated customers can capture savings at renewal.

caveats: The estimate does not assume autonomous control of hazardous processes.
RMP obligations apply only where regulated substances exceed thresholds, so compliance burden varies.
rho.change_evidence: Plant-level before-and-after deployments from specialty chemical formulators showing affected workflows, validation duration, exception rates, sustained labor hours removed, and safety or quality deviations over at least twelve months.
rho.rationale: Planning, document, customer-service, and inspection-assistance tools can be implemented within five years, but data fragmentation, small batch sizes, hazardous-process controls, formulation validation, customer qualification, and change-management requirements keep a substantial share of exposed work from becoming realized labor avoidance.

s5.bridge: Census ABS owner-age data describe responding owners of employer businesses broadly, not five-year qualifying control transfers of 325998 LMM firms. The range converts general succession exposure into a transfer probability and discounts internal succession, closures, corporate divisions, failed processes, and firms rendered nontransferable by liabilities. Broad owner data could overstate this code where corporate ownership is common or understate it where niche formulators are founder-led.
caveats: ABS includes industries and firm sizes outside the lens.
Closures and internal family succession are not qualifying transfers.
s5.change_evidence: A five-year census of PitchBook, Capital IQ, GF Data, Axial, broker, and court records mapped to a complete 325998 firm roster, with transactions classified by control, EBITDA, operating continuity, and environmental survival.
s5.rationale: A large estimated firm pool and founder-led niche manufacturing make some transfer flow plausible, but owner age alone is not a transaction and no complete six-digit deal series was located.

principal_driver: Qualified repeat specialty formulations can combine sticky physical demand with automatable planning, quality, documentation, and commercial workflows.
principal_weakness: A residual NAICS category masks radically different products, regulation, liabilities, demand paths, and contract economics.
